records.ety: [F. renom. See Noun, and cf. Renown, v.]

defs: The state of being much known and talked of; exalted reputation derived from the extensive praise of great achievements or accomplishments; fame; celebrity; -- always in a good sense.
quotes: Nor envy we Thy great renown, nor grudge thy victory.

defs: Report of nobleness or exploits; praise.
quotes: This famous duke of Milan, Of whom so often I have heard renown.

records.ety: [F. renommer to name again, celebrate, make famous; pref. re- re- + nommer to name, L. nominare , fr. nomen a name. See Noun.]

defs: To make famous; to give renown to.
quotes: For joy to hear me so renown his son.
The bard whom pilfered pastorals renown.
